title: Long call
marketView: Bullish directional move above strike plus premium.
volatilityView: Benefits from rising implied volatility before expiration.

payoff.maxLoss: Premium paid plus fees.

greekProfile.delta: Positive, increases as the call moves in the money.
greekProfile.gamma: Positive, larger near the money and near expiration.
greekProfile.theta: Negative; time decay works against the long option.
greekProfile.rho: Usually positive for calls, smaller than other Greeks for most retail horizons.

riskNotes: Defined-risk long premium trade, but a 100 percent premium loss is possible.
Avoid market orders on wide bid/ask contracts; use limit price and check open interest.
cliGuidance: Use options chain to pick expiration/strike, then options strategy-plan long-call with account_number, chain_id, expiration, long_call_option_id, limit_price, quantity, time_in_force, and ref_id.

title: Long put
marketView: Bearish directional move below strike minus premium, or protective hedge.
volatilityView: Benefits from rising implied volatility before expiration.

payoff.maxProfit: Substantial but capped by underlying going to zero: strike - net_debit.
payoff.maxLoss: Premium paid plus fees.

greekProfile.delta: Negative, becomes more negative as the put moves in the money.
greekProfile.gamma: Positive, larger near the money and near expiration.
greekProfile.theta: Negative; time decay works against the long option.

riskNotes: Defined-risk premium trade; max loss is the debit paid.
Check whether this is an opening speculation or a hedge against owned shares.
cliGuidance: Use the put chain and marketdata route first; then dry-run the single-leg debit order.

title: Sell to close an existing long option
marketView: Exit, trim, or take profit/loss on an existing long option.
volatilityView: Neutral; closes existing Greek exposure.

payoff.maxProfit: Exit value received minus opening cost.
payoff.maxLoss: No new opening risk if position_effect is close and quantity does not exceed held quantity.

greekProfile.delta: Reduces or removes the existing position delta.
greekProfile.gamma: Reduces or removes the existing long gamma.
greekProfile.theta: Stops paying theta on the closed contracts.
greekProfile.rho: Reduces or removes existing rho exposure.

legs.notes: optionType can be call or put; use the held instrument id.

riskNotes: Do not allow position_effect=open for this workflow.
Quantity must be less than or equal to the currently held long option quantity.
cliGuidance: Prefer this workflow for closing longs; validate held_option_id from aggregate_positions before dry-running options/orders.

title: Covered call
marketView: Neutral to moderately bullish on stock already owned; willing to sell shares at strike.
volatilityView: Benefits from time decay and lower implied volatility after entry.

payoff.maxProfit: Call credit plus stock appreciation up to strike.
payoff.maxLoss: Stock downside less credit; large if stock falls sharply.

greekProfile.delta: Net positive but less positive than long stock alone.
greekProfile.gamma: Short gamma from the call.
greekProfile.rho: Small; depends on call and stock financing context.

riskNotes: Covered means 100 shares per short call are already held in the same account.
Assignment can sell shares at the strike, including before expiration around dividends.
cliGuidance: Before planning the order, verify enough underlying shares in the target account. This is not a naked short-call workflow.

title: Cash-secured short put
marketView: Neutral to bullish; willing to buy shares at strike.
volatilityView: Benefits from time decay and lower implied volatility after entry.

payoff.maxLoss: Strike minus credit, if assigned and underlying goes to zero.

greekProfile.delta: Positive; short put behaves directionally bullish.
greekProfile.gamma: Short gamma.
greekProfile.rho: Usually positive for a short put.

riskNotes: Cash-secured means cash collateral is available for 100 shares per contract.
If not cash-secured, this becomes a margin short put with higher liquidation and assignment risk.
cliGuidance: Verify cash buying power and assignment willingness before any live write. Treat naked short puts as a different, higher-risk approval path.

title: Naked short call
marketView: Bearish to neutral; expects underlying not to rise above strike.
volatilityView: Benefits from time decay and lower implied volatility after entry.

payoff.maxLoss: Theoretically unlimited as underlying rises.

greekProfile.delta: Negative.
greekProfile.gamma: Short gamma; losses can accelerate as price rises.
greekProfile.rho: Usually negative for a short call.

riskNotes: Undefined-risk strategy; max loss is theoretically unlimited.
Requires exact user approval and margin/option-level validation before any live write.
Prefer a defined-risk call credit spread unless the user explicitly requests naked exposure.
cliGuidance: Keep this as an explicitly named aggressive strategy. Never infer naked short-call intent from a generic 'sell a call' request.

title: Call credit spread
marketView: Bearish to neutral; short lower strike call, long higher strike call.
volatilityView: Benefits from time decay and often from lower implied volatility.

payoff.maxLoss: Width between strikes minus net credit.

greekProfile.delta: Net negative.
greekProfile.gamma: Net short gamma, partially hedged by long call wing.

riskNotes: Defined-risk alternative to a naked short call.
Confirm long call strike is higher than short call strike and both expire on the same date.
cliGuidance: This matches the user-observed builder shape: sell lower call and buy higher call for a net credit. Use strategy quote route when available, otherwise compute mid from leg quotes.

title: Put credit spread
marketView: Bullish to neutral; short higher strike put, long lower strike put.
volatilityView: Benefits from time decay and often from lower implied volatility.

payoff.maxLoss: Width between strikes minus net credit.

greekProfile.delta: Net positive.
greekProfile.gamma: Net short gamma, partially hedged by long put wing.

riskNotes: Defined-risk alternative to a cash-secured or naked short put.
Confirm long put strike is lower than short put strike and both expire on the same date.
cliGuidance: Use for defined-risk bullish premium selling. Check options buying power before any live attempt.

title: Long straddle
marketView: Large move either direction; directionally neutral at entry.
volatilityView: Long volatility; benefits from rising implied volatility and realized movement.

payoff.maxProfit: Large upside if underlying moves far enough; put side capped by underlying going to zero.
payoff.maxLoss: Total net debit paid.

greekProfile.delta: Near zero at entry if at the money, then becomes directional as price moves.
greekProfile.gamma: Strong positive gamma.

riskNotes: Needs a large move or volatility expansion; theta decay can be severe.
Both legs should use same strike and expiration.
cliGuidance: Use for event-volatility research and dry-run only unless the user explicitly requests a live debit strategy.

title: Short straddle
marketView: Underlying stays near strike.
volatilityView: Short volatility; benefits from implied volatility collapse and time decay.

payoff.maxLoss: Large downside if underlying collapses and theoretically unlimited upside if underlying rallies.

greekProfile.delta: Near zero at entry, then becomes increasingly directional against the move.
greekProfile.gamma: Strong short gamma.

riskNotes: Undefined-risk short-volatility strategy with severe gap and assignment risk.
Never infer this from a generic 'sell premium' request; require exact strategy confirmation.
cliGuidance: Keep as research/planning unless the user explicitly asks for a live short straddle and all gates/eligibility checks pass.

title: Long call butterfly
marketView: Underlying pins near the middle strike at expiration.
volatilityView: Often benefits from time decay near the body and lower implied volatility.

payoff.maxProfit: Middle strike pin value minus net debit.
payoff.maxLoss: Net debit paid.

greekProfile.delta: Near neutral around body strike, directional if price moves away.
greekProfile.gamma: Can be positive or negative depending on spot relative to body and time.
greekProfile.theta: Usually positive near the body, negative far away.

riskNotes: All legs should share expiration and use evenly spaced strikes unless intentionally building a broken-wing butterfly.
Validate Robinhood accepts ratio_quantity 2 for the short body before any live send.
cliGuidance: Use as a dry-run strategy template first. If Robinhood rejects ratio_quantity 2, split into accepted combo order shape captured from Legend.

title: Iron condor
marketView: Range-bound underlying between short strikes.
volatilityView: Short volatility; benefits from time decay and lower implied volatility.

payoff.maxLoss: Wider wing width minus net credit.

greekProfile.delta: Near neutral if centered.
greekProfile.gamma: Short gamma, defined by long wings.

riskNotes: Defined-risk short-volatility trade, but max loss can still be many times the credit.
All legs must share expiration; long wings should be farther OTM than short strikes.
cliGuidance: Use for range-bound premium selling with explicit wing widths. Always calculate max loss and options buying power before live approval.

title: Call debit spread
marketView: Bullish move toward or above the short call strike.
volatilityView: Long directional debit with reduced vega compared with a standalone long call.

payoff.maxLoss: Net debit paid.

greekProfile.delta: Net positive, capped by the short call.
greekProfile.gamma: Positive near the long strike, reduced by the short call.
greekProfile.theta: Mixed; less negative than a standalone long call.

riskNotes: Defined-risk debit spread, but max profit is capped.
Both calls should share expiration; verify strikes are ordered lower long, higher short.
cliGuidance: Use when the user wants bullish defined-risk exposure instead of an outright long call.

title: Put debit spread
marketView: Bearish move toward or below the short put strike.
volatilityView: Long directional debit with reduced vega compared with a standalone long put.

payoff.maxLoss: Net debit paid.

greekProfile.delta: Net negative, capped by the short put.
greekProfile.gamma: Positive near the long strike, reduced by the short put.
greekProfile.theta: Mixed; less negative than a standalone long put.

riskNotes: Defined-risk debit spread, but max profit is capped.
Both puts should share expiration; verify strikes are ordered higher long, lower short.
cliGuidance: Use when the user wants bearish defined-risk exposure instead of an outright long put.

title: Naked or margin-secured short put
marketView: Neutral to bullish; willing or required to buy shares if assigned.
volatilityView: Short volatility; benefits from decay and lower implied volatility.

payoff.maxLoss: Large downside: strike minus credit if underlying goes to zero.

greekProfile.delta: Positive because a sold put benefits from upward price movement.
greekProfile.gamma: Negative; losses accelerate as the underlying falls.

riskNotes: Do not treat this as cash-secured unless the account has enough cash collateral and the user requested that exact structure.
Assignment, margin, and gap risk must be explicit in the dry-run summary.
cliGuidance: Use only when the user explicitly asks for margin/naked short-put exposure; otherwise prefer cash-secured put or put credit spread planning.

title: Covered put
marketView: Bearish to neutral; short stock plus short put.
volatilityView: Short volatility; benefits from decay, but keeps short-stock upside risk.

payoff.maxProfit: Short stock gain capped by the short put strike plus credit.
payoff.maxLoss: Upside risk from the short stock remains theoretically unlimited.

greekProfile.delta: Net negative from short stock, partly offset by the short put.
greekProfile.gamma: Negative from the short put.

riskNotes: Covered put is not the same as cash-secured put.
Requires an existing short stock position and keeps upside risk from that short stock.
cliGuidance: If the user says 'covered short put', ask whether they mean cash-secured put or covered put before building any order body.

title: Long strangle
marketView: Large move either direction beyond either strike plus total debit.
volatilityView: Long volatility; benefits from implied-volatility expansion.

payoff.maxProfit: Large upside on call side and substantial downside on put side.
payoff.maxLoss: Net debit paid.

greekProfile.delta: Near neutral if centered, turns directional after movement.
greekProfile.gamma: Positive.

riskNotes: Defined risk, but time decay can be heavy.
Requires the underlying to move enough to overcome both premiums.
cliGuidance: Use when the user wants long-volatility exposure but not necessarily same-strike straddle exposure.

title: Short strangle
marketView: Range-bound underlying between two short strikes.
volatilityView: Short volatility; benefits from time decay and lower implied volatility.

payoff.maxLoss: Undefined on the call side and large on the put side.

greekProfile.delta: Near neutral if centered, becomes directional when tested.
greekProfile.gamma: Negative.

riskNotes: Undefined-risk short-volatility strategy; do not infer from 'sell options' or 'strangle'.
Requires explicit user confirmation of naked short option exposure.
cliGuidance: Prefer iron condor if the user wants a defined-risk alternative; keep short strangle exact-approval only.

title: Call calendar roll
marketView: Move call exposure from one expiration/strike to another, usually by closing the existing call and opening a later-dated call.
volatilityView: Depends on the close/open legs; later-dated long calls usually add duration and vega.

payoff.maxProfit: Depends on the replacement call after the close leg; compute from the net debit or credit.
payoff.maxLoss: For a buy-to-open replacement call, max opening loss is the net debit paid after close proceeds.

greekProfile.delta: Compare closed call delta to opened call delta; rolling up usually reduces delta, rolling down usually increases it.
greekProfile.gamma: Closing a near-term call reduces near-term gamma; opening a later call usually adds lower gamma.
greekProfile.theta: Often reduces near-term theta burn but keeps negative theta if the replacement is long.
greekProfile.vega: Usually increases duration/vega when rolling to a later expiration.
greekProfile.rho: Small positive net exposure for long replacement calls.

riskNotes: Verify the close leg is actually held before calling this a roll.
For cash accounts, same-ticket or same-day replacement may fail if close proceeds are not settled; use options roll-plan --cash-account for a staged delayed roll.
Rolling can increase risk by adding duration, changing strike, or turning a closing trade into new opening exposure.
cliGuidance: Use options strategy-quote call-calendar-roll with --expiration as the default plus --param close_call_expiration=<old date> --param open_call_expiration=<new date>, or use options roll-plan --cash-account for staged close-today/open-next-business-day dry-runs.

title: Put calendar roll
marketView: Move put exposure from one expiration/strike to another, usually by closing the existing put and opening a later-dated put.
volatilityView: Depends on the close/open legs; later-dated long puts usually add duration and vega.

payoff.maxProfit: Depends on the replacement put after the close leg; compute from the net debit or credit.
payoff.maxLoss: For a buy-to-open replacement put, max opening loss is the net debit paid after close proceeds.

greekProfile.delta: Compare closed put delta to opened put delta; replacement puts usually keep negative directional exposure.
greekProfile.gamma: Closing a near-term put reduces near-term gamma; opening a later put usually adds lower gamma.
greekProfile.theta: Often reduces near-term theta burn but keeps negative theta if the replacement is long.
greekProfile.vega: Usually increases duration/vega when rolling to a later expiration.
greekProfile.rho: Small negative net exposure for long replacement puts.

riskNotes: Verify the close leg is actually held before calling this a roll.
For cash accounts, same-ticket or same-day replacement may fail if close proceeds are not settled; use options roll-plan --cash-account for a staged delayed roll.
Rolling can increase risk by adding duration, changing strike, or turning a closing trade into new opening exposure.
cliGuidance: Use options strategy-quote put-calendar-roll with --expiration as the default plus --param close_put_expiration=<old date> --param open_put_expiration=<new date>, or use options roll-plan --cash-account for staged close-today/open-next-business-day dry-runs.
